news.summary: In Denver, Tallgrass Pony Express Pipeline, LLC (Pony Express) under Tallgrass management, in conjunction with Bridger Pipeline, LLC, has revealed a fresh binding joint tariff open season. This decision offers incentive tariff rates for shippers willing to commit to transporting crude oil from Bridger's Guernsey Hub. The initiative also presents the opportunity for Pony Express to grow by around 22,000 barrels per day, subject to stakeholder demand. Scheduled to begin on May 9, 2025, the open season will extend for 30 days, allowing prospective shippers to participate.
news.headline: Tallgrass and Bridger Pipeline announce open season for Pony Express Pipeline, offering incentive tariff rates for crude oil transportation. Expansion potential of 22,000 barrels per day based on shipper interest. Open season runs for 30 days from May 9, 2025.

news.summary: In 2022, a scam involving the theft of 14 Bored Ape non-fungible tokens (NFTs) worth over $1 million took place. Cybersecurity researcher ZachXBT identified the wallet behind the scam, leading to the detention of Sam Curry, a former security researcher at Yuga Labs, by law enforcement in 2023. Curry was wrongly detained as a suspect, as he was investigating the theft and using a private key put in the website by the threat actor as part of his security work. ZachXBT used forensic tracing to identify a likely suspect in the theft and urged law enforcement to investigate social media accounts and on-chain transactions. Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs are highly valuable, with a market cap exceeding $300 million. As of May 9, a single NFT traded for around $30,000 on OpenSea, but in 2022, each NFT was valued at roughly $86,000. The mistaken arrest highlights the complexities and risks in investigating digital crimes.
news.headline: ZachXBT reveals wrong suspect detained in Bored Ape NFT theft of over $1M; forensic tracing identifies likely culprit. Bored Ape NFTs valued at $30K each.

news.summary: Bitcoin's price is significantly influenced by global liquidity, with liquidity explaining up to 90% of its price movements. Raoul Pal notes that as liquidity continues to expand due to increasing debt levels in many countries, Bitcoin's price is likely to be driven further. The current liquidity cycle is projected to peak by mid-2026, with Bitcoin's price being sensitive to global liquidity trends and experiencing near-perfect correlation with it, with a lag of about three months. The growth in global liquidity is mainly driven by expanding money supply through ever-growing debt levels in fiat currency systems. Michael Howell believes global liquidity moves in roughly five-year cycles and predicts the current cycle to mature by mid-2026. Despite uncertainty over economic conditions and potential downturns, liquidity is expected to continue rising at least through 2025. The alignment between global liquidity cycles and Bitcoin's halving cycles suggests a potential major price move in the near future. The relationship between global liquidity and Bitcoin's price highlights the significance of liquidity expansion in driving the value of various assets, including Bitcoin.
news.headline: Bitcoin price surges with global liquidity growth; up to 90% of its price movements explained by liquidity. Analysts predict mid-2026 peak. BTC nears $100,000 fueled by liquidity, aligning with global M2. Expansion driven by debt levels. Global liquidity tied to US Fed, PBoC, and lending. Cycle poised to peak by mid-2026, setting stage for downturn. Despite potential recession, liquidity to remain strong through 2025, impacting Bitcoin's rise. Headwinds expected as bond market tightens. Policy response likely to boost liquidity, supporting hedges like gold, equities, real estate, and Bitcoin. Potential convergence of liquidity and Bitcoin halving cycles in late 2025/early 2026 could trigger major price move. No investment advice provided. Conduct own research before decisions.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, in Grand Junction, Colorado, ProStar Holdings Inc. (OTCQB: MAPPF) (TSXV: MAPS) (FSE: 5D00), a global leader in Precision Mapping Solutions ®, successfully closed a non-brokered private placement offering. The company raised C$775,000 in gross proceeds by selling 5,535,714 units at a price of $0.14 per unit. This amount exceeded the initial target of C$750,000, as approved by the TSX Venture Exchange. The increase in the offering size was authorized just before the closing of the funding round, as per the announcement made on May 5, 2025.
news.headline: ProStar Holdings closes upsized non-brokered private placement raising $775,000 through sale of 5,535,714 units at $0.14/unit.

news.summary: Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation (NYSE: VAC) exceeded expectations in the first quarter by reporting an adjusted EPS of $1.66 per share, surpassing the analyst prediction of $1.49 per share. However, their quarterly sales amounted to $1.20 billion, slightly below the anticipated $1.21 billion. The company remains positive about its performance, citing growth in first-time buyer sales and Adjusted EBITDA. As a result, they have raised their FY25 adjusted EPS guidance. John Geller, the President and CEO of the corporation, expressed satisfaction with the strong first-quarter results and emphasized the effectiveness of their leisure-focused business model.
news.headline: Marriott Vacations Worldwide exceeds Q1 expectations with $1.66 EPS but misses on sales with $1.20 billion. CEO cites leisure model success.

news.summary: Democratic lawmakers have expressed concern over alleged financial connections between cryptocurrency platform Binance and ventures associated with the Trump family. A $2 billion deal involving a digital token supported by Trump has raised eyebrows. Senators Chris Van Hollen and Elizabeth Warren have reached out to top Treasury and Justice Department officials for more information on the matter's implications for federal oversight of Binance. Reports indicate that USD1, a stablecoin linked to a Trump-affiliated company, was utilized in March to settle a significant investment by Abu Dhabi's MXG into Binance. This transaction, made public on May 1, has brought about increased scrutiny.
news.headline: Senate Dems probe Binance-Trump stablecoin ties after $2B deal; Senators ask officials about implications, $1 stablecoin use in Abu Dhabi investment.

news.summary: Experience the ability to venture into distant areas and capture elusive Pokémon without physically manipulating your phone or changing your location. With the Tenorshare iAnyGo Auto-Catch, users can now effortlessly engage in Pokémon hunts from afar without the need to physically move. The latest update to the iAnyGo Auto-Catch introduces enhanced features, including compatibility with the advanced Go Plus device.
news.headline: New Tenorshare iAnyGo update allows Pokemon GO players to spoof locations and auto-catch Pokemon, enhancing gameplay.

news.summary: Bitcoin has surged to $104,000, a level not seen since February 7th, marking an important milestone. Ethereum has experienced a significant increase of over 25% following the Pectra upgrade, which has made ETH deflationary once again. In the last 24 hours, approximately $800 million worth of short positions have been liquidated. Renowned investor Raoul Pal suggests that Bitcoin dominance may have reached its peak. Missouri announced plans to fully exempt taxes on cryptocurrency gains. Unfortunately, the GENIUS Act did not pass the Senate vote. There is a new strategy in place to introduce the 'Bitcoin Standard Model'. Additionally, Strategy stock is at year-to-date highs while Fidelity is actively promoting Bitcoin to CEOs. Standard Chartered believes that Bitcoin surpassing the $120,000 mark may be underestimated. The Ripple SEC case has been officially closed. Meta is exploring the development and deployment of stablecoins. Coinbase is set to acquire Deribit for $2.9 billion, though its recent earnings have fallen short of expectations. A superstate is working on tokenized stocks for Solana. The founder of Celsius has been sentenced to 12 years in prison for fraud. Ramp is strategizing the introduction of corporate stablecoin-backed cards.
news.headline: Cryptos surge after key upgrades. BTC hits $104k, ETH up 25% post-Pectra. $800m shorts closed, MO exempts crypto tax. BTC at $120k? Ripple case concluded. Meta eyes stablecoins. Coinbase, Deribit deal at $2.9bn. Celsius founder sentenced for fraud.
